displayTitle: The Film Owes You Three Proofs

beats.narration: Before you call a single flaw, the film owes you three proofs: coverage, density, and honesty. Let's collect them.

payload.title: THREE PROOFS
payload.sub: What every radiograph owes you before interpretation.

beats.narration: Coverage first. Lead location markers map film to part, and abutting films must share a marker image. No shared marker? That length is unproven.

payload.heading: Markers prove complete coverage
payload.note: NO SHARED MARKER = UNPROVEN = NOT EXAMINED

beats.narration: Never eyeball density. A verified densitometer reads the I Q I body, and every weld reading stays within minus fifteen, plus thirty percent of it.

payload.heading: Read the IQI body, window the weld
payload.note: WINDOW: -15% TO +30% OF IQI BODY DENSITY

beats.narration: Artifacts are images that never came from the part. Tilt the film, compare the second film, check the part itself — when in doubt, reshoot.
So land the rule: this film is evidence. Prove coverage, measure density, rule out every artifact — then, and only then, interpret.

payload.sub: Coverage proven · density measured · artifacts ruled out
